zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Hebert於1428年出生在英國史洛普爾布福德時，她的父親Edmund Cornewall46歲，母親Elizabeth Barre15歲。她和Sir Hugh de Mortimer Lord of Kyre and Martley至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1519年12月23日在英國英格蘭賀福爾卡芙特享年91歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭賀福爾卡芙特。

nameMeanings.name: Hebert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Emile, Normand, Jacques, Marcel, Pierre, Lucien, Michel, Gaston, Alcide, Camille.
French (Hébert): assimilated form of Herbert . Compare Abair , Abar , Abare , Abear , Aber , Eber , and Ebert .

variants.name: Herbert
meanings: German, English, and French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements heri, hari ‘army’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. In Britain, this Old French name, introduced by the Normans, reinforced the less common Old English cognate Herebeorht. The surname was taken to Ireland after the Anglo-Norman invasion and in the 16th century.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the German personal name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Abar
meanings: Possibly also Basque: topographic name from abar ‘branch(es), firewood’. This surname is very rare in Spain.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Hebert was born in 1428, in Burford, Shropshire, England, her father, Edmund Cornewall, was 46 and her mother, Elizabeth Barre, was 15. She had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with Sir Hugh de Mortimer Lord of Kyre and Martley. She died on 23 December 1519, in Croft, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 91, and was buried in Croft, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec Sir Hugh de Mortimer Lord of Kyre and Martley. Elle est décédée le 23 décembre 1519, à Croft, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 91 ans, et a été enterrée à Croft, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Hebertが1428年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアブルフォードで生まれた時，父Edmund Cornewallは46歳，母Elizabeth Barreは15歳であった。彼女はSir Hugh de Mortimer Lord of Kyre and Martleyとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1519年12月23日にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアクロフトで91歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアクロフトにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Hebert(이)가 1428년 영국 슈럽실르 버퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edmund Cornewall(은)는 46세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Barre(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 Sir Hugh de Mortimer Lord of Kyre and Martley(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1519년 12월 23일 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 크로프트에서 91세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 크로프트에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Hebert родилась в 1428 г. в местности бурфорд, шропшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Edmund Cornewall было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Barre, 15. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь от Sir Hugh de Mortimer Lord of Kyre and Martley. Она умерла 23 декабря 1519 г. в местности крофт, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 91 год и была похоронена в местности крофт, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания.
